[[JA:男乃宇刀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578102}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Onouto Shrine |画像=[[File:Onouto jinja3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q696412|WD=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}Hotokenami-cho 1740 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Itsuse-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato-Iwarehiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}
{{ill|lt=Inishikiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11372194|WD=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Onouto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Onouto Shrine''' (おのうとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hotokenami-cho|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q696412|WD=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}[https://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/naniwa/publication/book18.pdf#search='%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E3%82%92%E4%B8%AD%E5%BF%83%E3%81%A8%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B%E6%9D%91%E8%90%BD%E7%94%9F%E6%B4%BB%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%EF%BC%89' Kansai University Naniwa/Osaka Cultural Heritage Research Center, Village Life Survey Centered on Shrines (3), Osaka Prefecture], viewed 2018-12-02. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deities * {{ill|lt=Itsuse-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato-Iwarehiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|lt=Inishikiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11372194|WD=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}} === History === In the name "Onouto," "Ono" means elder brother and "Uto" means younger brother, referring to enshrining elder brother {{ill|lt=Itsuse-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto}} and younger brother {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato-Iwarehiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. According to shrine tradition, when Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto was wounded in battle with {{ill|lt=Nagasunehiko|Q4922278|WD=Q4922278|1=Nagatsuhiko}}, a local chieftain Yokoyama-hiko welcomed him here. It is an Engishiki shrine, and because of ties to the local ruler in the Genkei era, {{ill|lt=Inishikiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11372194|WD=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}} also came to be enshrined. Two mikoshi descend the steep steps of the shrine building and circulate through the district''{{ill|lt=#Izumi City Fudoki 2|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumi City Fudoki (2)}}'', opening gravure (photos and commentary).. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q696412|WD=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hotokenami-cho|1=UNKNOWN}}1740 ;Access * From {{ill|lt=Izumi-Chuo Station#Other_2|Q6102264|WD=Q6102264|lt=Izumi-Chuo Station|1=Izumi-Chūō Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nankai Semboku Line|Q2758012|WD=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}}, take Izumi City {{ill|lt=Meguru#Route-maintenance bus|Q11279660|WD=Q11279660|lt=route-maintenance bus|1=route-maintenance bus}} ({{ill|lt=Meguru#Chichioni route|Q11279660|WD=Q11279660|lt=Chichioni route|1=Chichioni route}}) bound for "Makio Yamaguchi" or "Chichioni," get off at "Jinja-mae," immediately nearby. * From Izumi-Chuo Station on the Nankai Semboku Line, take {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus|Q11408154|WD=Q11408154|1=Nankai Bus}} {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus Komyoike Office#Sakamoto Line|Q11408155|WD=Q11408155|lt=Sakamoto Line|1=Sakamoto Line}} or {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus Komyoike Office#Hatsugano Line|Q11408155|WD=Q11408155|lt=Hatsugano Line|1=Hatsugano Line}}, get off at "Nobana" or "Izumi Aobadai," transfer to reservation shared-ride service "{{ill|lt=Choisoko|Q107253531|WD=Q107253531|1=Choisoko}} Izumi," and get off at "704 Jinja-mae" {{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/kakukano/dezainbu/tosiseisaku/gyoumu/koutsuukankei/public_transportation/access_bus/19627.html |title=Guide to "Choisoko Izumi" |publisher=Izumi City |access-date=2025-06-07 }} . === Footnotes === === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *{{Cite book|和書|editor=Masao Inoue|title=Osaka Prefecture Complete Gazetteer|year=1922|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Complete Gazetteer Publishing Office|volume=Vol.5|language=Japanese|id={{NDLJP|965802}}|pages=618-619|ref=Osaka Prefecture Complete Gazetteer Vol.5}} [[JA:男乃宇刀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578102}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Onouto Shrine |画像=[[File:Onouto jinja3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q696412|WD=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}Hotokenami-cho 1740 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Itsuse-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato-Iwarehiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}
{{ill|lt=Inishikiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11372194|WD=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Onouto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Onouto Shrine''' (おのうとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hotokenami-cho|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q696412|WD=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}[https://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/naniwa/publication/book18.pdf#search='%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E3%82%92%E4%B8%AD%E5%BF%83%E3%81%A8%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B%E6%9D%91%E8%90%BD%E7%94%9F%E6%B4%BB%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%EF%BC%89' Kansai University Naniwa/Osaka Cultural Heritage Research Center, Village Life Survey Centered on Shrines (3), Osaka Prefecture], viewed 2018-12-02. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deities * {{ill|lt=Itsuse-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato-Iwarehiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|lt=Inishikiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11372194|WD=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}} === History === In the name "Onouto," "Ono" means elder brother and "Uto" means younger brother, referring to enshrining elder brother {{ill|lt=Itsuse-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto}} and younger brother {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato-Iwarehiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. According to shrine tradition, when Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto was wounded in battle with {{ill|lt=Nagasunehiko|Q4922278|WD=Q4922278|1=Nagatsuhiko}}, a local chieftain Yokoyama-hiko welcomed him here. It is an Engishiki shrine, and because of ties to the local ruler in the Genkei era, {{ill|lt=Inishikiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11372194|WD=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}} also came to be enshrined. Two mikoshi descend the steep steps of the shrine building and circulate through the district''{{ill|lt=#Izumi City Fudoki 2|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumi City Fudoki (2)}}'', opening gravure (photos and commentary).. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q696412|WD=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hotokenami-cho|1=UNKNOWN}}1740 ;Access * From {{ill|lt=Izumi-Chuo Station#Other_2|Q6102264|WD=Q6102264|lt=Izumi-Chuo Station|1=Izumi-Chūō Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nankai Semboku Line|Q2758012|WD=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}}, take Izumi City {{ill|lt=Meguru#Route-maintenance bus|Q11279660|WD=Q11279660|lt=route-maintenance bus|1=route-maintenance bus}} ({{ill|lt=Meguru#Chichioni route|Q11279660|WD=Q11279660|lt=Chichioni route|1=Chichioni route}}) bound for "Makio Yamaguchi" or "Chichioni," get off at "Jinja-mae," immediately nearby. * From Izumi-Chuo Station on the Nankai Semboku Line, take {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus|Q11408154|WD=Q11408154|1=Nankai Bus}} {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus Komyoike Office#Sakamoto Line|Q11408155|WD=Q11408155|lt=Sakamoto Line|1=Sakamoto Line}} or {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus Komyoike Office#Hatsugano Line|Q11408155|WD=Q11408155|lt=Hatsugano Line|1=Hatsugano Line}}, get off at "Nobana" or "Izumi Aobadai," transfer to reservation shared-ride service "{{ill|lt=Choisoko|Q107253531|WD=Q107253531|1=Choisoko}} Izumi," and get off at "704 Jinja-mae" {{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/kakukano/dezainbu/tosiseisaku/gyoumu/koutsuukankei/public_transportation/access_bus/19627.html |title=Guide to "Choisoko Izumi" |publisher=Izumi City |access-date=2025-06-07 }} . === Footnotes === === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *{{Cite book|和書|editor=Masao Inoue|title=Osaka Prefecture Complete Gazetteer|year=1922|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Complete Gazetteer Publishing Office|volume=Vol.5|language=Japanese|id={{NDLJP|965802}}|pages=618-619|ref=Osaka Prefecture Complete Gazetteer Vol.5}} [[commons:Category:Onouto jinja]][[ja:男乃宇刀神社]] {{Expand Japanese|男乃宇刀神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Onouto Shrine | native_name = 男乃宇刀神社 | image = Onouto jinja3.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Onouto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.4217|135.487284}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Itsuse no Mikoto|lt=Hiko Itsuse no Mikoto|en|Itsuse no Mikoto|fr|Hikoitsuse no Mikoto|ja|彦五瀬命|ko|이쓰세노미코토|pl|Itsuse|zh|彥五瀨命|WD=Q10881970|1=Itsuse no Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Onouto Shrine'''|男乃宇刀神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Onouto Shrine''' (おのうとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hotokenami-cho|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q696412|WD=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}[https://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/naniwa/publication/book18.pdf#search='%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E3%82%92%E4%B8%AD%E5%BF%83%E3%81%A8%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B%E6%9D%91%E8%90%BD%E7%94%9F%E6%B4%BB%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%EF%BC%89' Kansai University Naniwa/Osaka Cultural Heritage Research Center, Village Life Survey Centered on Shrines (3), Osaka Prefecture], viewed 2018-12-02. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deities * {{ill|lt=Itsuse-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato-Iwarehiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|lt=Inishikiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11372194|WD=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}} === History === In the name "Onouto," "Ono" means elder brother and "Uto" means younger brother, referring to enshrining elder brother {{ill|lt=Itsuse-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto}} and younger brother {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato-Iwarehiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. According to shrine tradition, when Hikoitsuse-no-Mikoto was wounded in battle with {{ill|lt=Nagasunehiko|Q4922278|WD=Q4922278|1=Nagatsuhiko}}, a local chieftain Yokoyama-hiko welcomed him here. It is an Engishiki shrine, and because of ties to the local ruler in the Genkei era, {{ill|lt=Inishikiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11372194|WD=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}} also came to be enshrined. Two mikoshi descend the steep steps of the shrine building and circulate through the district''{{ill|lt=#Izumi City Fudoki 2|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumi City Fudoki (2)}}'', opening gravure (photos and commentary).. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q696412|WD=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hotokenami-cho|1=UNKNOWN}}1740 ;Access * From {{ill|lt=Izumi-Chuo Station#Other_2|Q6102264|WD=Q6102264|lt=Izumi-Chuo Station|1=Izumi-Chūō Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nankai Semboku Line|Q2758012|WD=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}}, take Izumi City {{ill|lt=Meguru#Route-maintenance bus|Q11279660|WD=Q11279660|lt=route-maintenance bus|1=route-maintenance bus}} ({{ill|lt=Meguru#Chichioni route|Q11279660|WD=Q11279660|lt=Chichioni route|1=Chichioni route}}) bound for "Makio Yamaguchi" or "Chichioni," get off at "Jinja-mae," immediately nearby. * From Izumi-Chuo Station on the Nankai Semboku Line, take {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus|Q11408154|WD=Q11408154|1=Nankai Bus}} {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus Komyoike Office#Sakamoto Line|Q11408155|WD=Q11408155|lt=Sakamoto Line|1=Sakamoto Line}} or {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus Komyoike Office#Hatsugano Line|Q11408155|WD=Q11408155|lt=Hatsugano Line|1=Hatsugano Line}}, get off at "Nobana" or "Izumi Aobadai," transfer to reservation shared-ride service "{{ill|lt=Choisoko|Q107253531|WD=Q107253531|1=Choisoko}} Izumi," and get off at "704 Jinja-mae" {{Cite web |url=https://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/kakukano/dezainbu/tosiseisaku/gyoumu/koutsuukankei/public_transportation/access_bus/19627.html |title=Guide to "Choisoko Izumi" |publisher=Izumi City
